summary: My family and I live in Minnesota today, which is where our journey finally settled after many years of moving through different countries. Minnesota represents a place of stability and home for us, especially after the hardships we faced in refugee camps. I cherish the fact that my younger siblings now have a place to call home here, something I didn’t have growing up.

Before coming to Minnesota, my family and I lived in Kenya for some time as part of our journey as refugees. Kenya was one of the places where we sought safety and tried to rebuild our lives after fleeing Somalia. It was a temporary stop on our long path toward finding a permanent home.

Tanzania was another country where my family and I lived before eventually settling in Minnesota. Like Kenya and Ethiopia, Tanzania was part of our route as refugees searching for safety and stability. Each place held its own challenges and memories as we moved from one to another.

Ethiopia was one of the countries where my family and I stayed during our years of displacement. It was part of the long journey we took after leaving Somalia, before finally arriving in Minnesota. Living in Ethiopia was a chapter in our story of survival and hope.

Banadir refugee camp holds my childhood memories because I don’t remember much from Somalia itself. I was six years old in 1997 when a devastating fire broke out in the camp, destroying everything my family owned and many lives were lost. That experience shaped my early years and is a vivid part of my past, even though it was filled with loss and hardship.
